perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information,” as used herein, means all information provided by ABRH to the Company and its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Potential Transaction, including the existence of this Agreement and the discussions or negotiations regarding the above transaction, and the whole or any portion or phase of any scientific or technical information, invention, innovation, design, process, procedure, specification, formula, improvement, business or financial information, equipment, listing of names, addresses, or telephone numbers, or other information relating to ABRH’s business or profession which is secret and of value, regardless of form, including but not limited to (a) pricing; (b) balance statements, profit and loss statements, store operating cash flow statements, business/financial models and projections, forecasts, loan documents/financing terms, and other financial documents; (c) research, development, algorithms, data, studies, and know-how; (d) strategies, operations, methods, planning, products, recipes, and menus; (e) product compounds, types, shapes, devices, parts, and materials; (f) product development techniques or technologies, methods of synthesis, modeling and coding information, and packaging procedures; (g) shelf life goals, plant equipment/machinery, design and capacities, manufacturing processes, product volume, and distribution and logistical strategies and practices, including but not limited to freight charges, communications, deliveries, and transportation processes and arrangements; (h) markets, sales and cost data and sales and marketing techniques, technologies, processes, 400 West 48th Avenue Denver CO 80216 303.296.2121 procedures, artwork/creative, materials, videos, and productions; (i) product development, design information, and product ingredient usage and techniques; (j) franchise objectives, arrangements, structures, agreements, relationships, fees, royalties, contributions, marketing techniques, processes and procedures, and cooperative obligations; (k) leases and subleases and the terms of any agreement and the discussions, negotiations, and proposals related to that agreement or lease or sublease, including the parties’ identities; (l) software and hardware configuration, information, and capacities; (m) correspondences, summaries, abstracts, surveys, plans, drawings, insurance policies, and intellectual properties, including but not limited to trademarks, patents, and copyrights; (n) pending claims, disputes, and party resolutions; (o) employee background information and personal identification, employment histories, resumes, and payroll information; (p) employee benefit plans, training materials, policies, and procedures; (q) all analyses or other documents prepared by ABRH, FNH, the Company, or any of their affiliated entities, members/shareholders/partners, investors, managers, directors, officers, employees, advisors, attorneys, accountants, consultants, subcontractors, representatives, or affiliates, which contain or otherwise reflect information not to be disclosed herein, or has been marked as “Confidential”; and (r) the proprietary or confidential information of any third party who may disclose such information to a party in the course of such party’s business.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information,” as used herein, unless explicitly marked otherwise, means only pricing information provided by ABRH to the Company and its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Potential Transaction. All other information, including but not limited to business plans, financial records, employee data, technical specifications, or any other material shared during negotiations is considered 'General Business Information' and is not subject to the same confidentiality restrictions outlined for 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of 'Confidential Information' drastically narrows its scope to *only* pricing information, while introducing the new term 'General Business Information' which is explicitly excluded from the stricter confidentiality protections. This change directly contradicts the later sections that treat ALL information provided by ABRH as confidential unless a specific exclusion applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure of Confidential Information . The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Confidential Information confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only pricing information, explicitly excluding other information as 'General Business Information' not subject to the same confidentiality. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which treats *all* information provided by ABRH as Confidential Information, subject to strict non-disclosure obligations, unless explicitly permitted. Therefore, the changed definition directly clashes with the broader confidentiality requirement outlined in the contradicted section, creating a clear in-text contradiction as described in the 'Perturbation Explanation'.

perturbation.original_text: Term . Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) two years from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Term . Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement related to Pricing Information shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) one year from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any failure to consummate the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The term limit is reduced to one year and it applies ONLY to pricing information. This contradicts the initial 'term' definition by creating an in-text contradiction within the original paragraph if there are other obligations that have longer time periods
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term . Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement related to Pricing Information shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) one year from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any failure to consummate the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the term limit to one year and restricts it to pricing information only. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but it doesn't address other potential obligations within the original agreement that were initially covered by a two-year term. The explanation clarifies that this creates an in-text contradiction if other obligations have longer time periods, because the new term only applies to pricing information but leaves the original term clause unaddressed and potentially applicable to other obligations, thus creating a contradiction. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists, as the change creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding obligations beyond pricing information.

perturbation.original_text: Non-disclosure of Confidential Information . The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Confidential Information confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-disclosure of Protected Data . The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Protected Data confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Protected Data, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Protected Data, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Protected Data by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Protected Data to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Protected Data for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Protected Data to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Protected Data confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Protected Data from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Protected Data from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in Section 3, without defining 'Protected Data' anywhere in the agreement, creates ambiguity. This contradicts the initial definition of 'Confidential Information' (Section 1) and makes it unclear which set of data the non-disclosure obligations apply to. There's no definition of Protected Data in the file, to ensure no confusion of what it means
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information,” as used herein, unless explicitly marked otherwise, means only pricing information provided by ABRH to the Company and its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Potential Transaction. All other information, including but not limited to business plans, financial records, employee data, technical specifications, or any other material shared during negotiations is considered 'General Business Information' and is not subject to the same confidentiality restrictions outlined for 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in Section 3. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Confidential Information' as only pricing information. The change therefore contradicts this definition by implying a different, undefined meaning of 'Protected Data', leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about what information is protected under the non-disclosure agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as the contract uses a new term ('Protected Data') without defining it, creating a conflict with the existing definition of 'Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: The term, “Confidential Information”, does not include, and the Company has no obligation to protect information which is (i) known to or acquired by the Company before disclosure under this Agreement; (ii) independently developed by the Company without relying on ABRH’s Confidential Information; (iii) or becomes part of the public domain (other than as a result of disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement) or lawfully obtained from a third party who is not, to the Company’s knowledge, under an obligation of confidentiality to ABRH with respect to such information; (iv) free of confidentiality restrictions by written agreement of ABRH; or (v) required to be disclosed by any law, government regulation, or judicial or other governmental order, provided that, if legally permissible, the Company provides reasonable advance written notice to ABRH to afford ABRH the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the Company becomes legally obligated or receives a subpoena or other legal demand issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body to disclose any Confidential Information, it shall cooperate with ABRH in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy, and shall use reasonable efforts to protect the confidential and proprietary status of any disclosed Confidential Information. ABRH will reimburse the Company and its Representatives for reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.changed_text: The term, “Restricted Data”, does not include, and the Company has no obligation to protect information which is (i) known to or acquired by the Company before disclosure under this Agreement; (ii) independently developed by the Company without relying on ABRH’s Restricted Data; (iii) or becomes part of the public domain (other than as a result of disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement) or lawfully obtained from a third party who is not, to the Company’s knowledge, under an obligation of confidentiality to ABRH with respect to such information; (iv) free of confidentiality restrictions by written agreement of ABRH; or (v) required to be disclosed by any law, government regulation, or judicial or other governmental order, provided that, if legally permissible, the Company provides reasonable advance written notice to ABRH to afford ABRH the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the Company becomes legally obligated or receives a subpoena or other legal demand issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body to disclose any Restricted Data, it shall cooperate with ABRH in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy, and shall use reasonable efforts to protect the confidential and proprietary status of any disclosed Restricted Data. ABRH will reimburse the Company and its Representatives for reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Restricted Data' in the Exclusions section, while other sections still refer to 'Confidential Information' or 'Protected Data', creates confusion about which exclusions apply to which type of data. Since it is still not defined anywhere 'Restricted Data' as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure of Protected Data . The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Protected Data confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Protected Data, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Protected Data, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Protected Data by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Protected Data to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Protected Data for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Protected Data to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Protected Data confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Protected Data from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Protected Data from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Restricted Data' in the definition of exclusions, while the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Protected Data'. Because other sections still use 'Confidential Information' or 'Protected Data', this creates a misalignment in terminology. The document no longer clearly defines what data is excluded from the non-disclosure obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text', as 'Protected Data' is now distinct from 'Restricted Data', potentially leading to inconsistent enforcement and ambiguity regarding which exclusions apply to each type of data.

perturbation.original_text: Return of Materials . Confidential Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction. Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Materials . General Business Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction. Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Pricing Data, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Pricing Data that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that General Business Information can be used during the potential transaction, but that only Pricing Data has to be return upon the request of ABRH, creates an in-text contradiction, and an uncertainty of what needs to be return, and what obligations Company has to protect it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . General Business Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction. Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Pricing Data, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Pricing Data that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Confidential Information must be returned, whereas the changed text changes 'Confidential Information' to 'General Business Information' in the first sentence and 'Pricing Data' in the remainder of the paragraph. The contradiction is that while the Company can use General Business Information for evaluating the Potential Transaction, only Pricing Data needs to be returned upon request. This contradicts an implicit requirement to return all Confidential Information. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition. The Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: Irreparable Injury; Injunctive Relief . The Company acknowledges that any unauthorized or wrongful disclosure or use of Confidential Information by the Company, including the Company’s Representatives, or any other breach by the Company, may result in irreparable injury to ABRH that is not adequately compensable in monetary damages. Accordingly, the Company acknowledges that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, ABRH shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need to post any bond and in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity. The Company will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law in the event that ABRH seeks injunctive relief in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Irreparable Injury; Injunctive Relief . The Company acknowledges that any unauthorized or wrongful disclosure or use of Protected Financials by the Company, including the Company’s Representatives, or any other breach by the Company, may result in irreparable injury to ABRH that is not adequately compensable in monetary damages. Accordingly, the Company acknowledges that in the event of a breach of this Agreement *related to pricing data*, ABRH shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need to post any bond and in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity. The Company will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law in the event that ABRH seeks injunctive relief in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The paragraph mentions the term 'Protected Financials' that has not been defined in the contract, creating confusion. Also, the paragraph, makes the breach of the contract only enforceable to 'pricing data', creating an in-text contradiction in the original text, since any breach, does not only imply a breach related to pricing data. This creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcing any other provision in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Irreparable Injury; Injunctive Relief . The Company acknowledges that any unauthorized or wrongful disclosure or use of Protected Financials by the Company, including the Company’s Representatives, or any other breach by the Company, may result in irreparable injury to ABRH that is not adequately compensable in monetary damages. Accordingly, the Company acknowledges that in the event of a breach of this Agreement *related to pricing data*, ABRH shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need to post any bond and in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity. The Company will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law in the event that ABRH seeks injunctive relief in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'any other breach by the Company, may result in irreparable injury to ABRH'. However, the changed text limits this to 'a breach of this Agreement related to pricing data'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates a clear contradiction because it limits the scope of breaches that can result in irreparable injury, conflicting with the original broader statement about 'any other breach'. Also the undefined term 'Protected Financials' is misaligned terminology, which causes an in-text contradiction.
